positional_bias.note: 0.40 is not strictly greater than 0.40; threshold is > 0.40, so this passes.

length_bias.note: Only Q3 exceeds the 1.20 ratio (1.262). Rate 1/5 = 0.20 is well within the 0.40 ceiling. Previous fail at 0.80 resolved by shortening Q1 and Q2 correct answers and lengthening Q3 D2, Q4 D1 and D3.

weakest_questions.distractor: The values in the vector are converted to negative numbers and the algorithm rejects them as invalid
weakest_questions.note: Borderline 2/3 — not anchored to a common beginner misconception about position-swapping, but not absurd.

coverage_detail.lo: LO1 — Define a vector as an ordered list of scalars, where each position captures a different property of the same thing.
LO2 — Explain why grouping multiple properties into one vector is more useful than keeping them as separate, unrelated numbers.
LO3 — Construct a simple feature vector for a familiar object by deciding which properties to include and assigning a number to each.
LO4 — Describe how AI systems use feature vectors to represent real-world things so that patterns can be found through arithmetic.
LO5 — Identify the number of elements in a vector (its dimension) and explain why the same position must always mean the same property across all vectors being compared.

objective_coverage.note: LO3 previously uncovered. Now addressed by replacement Q5 — student constructs [160, 5, 65] for a cup of coffee given fixed slots (caffeine_mg, sugar_g, temperature_c).

beginner_fairness_and_scope.note: All questions testable from 6.3 corpus alone. Matrix appears only as name-and-defer in Q1-D2, consistent with corpus §8 and §5.1. Q5 uses real units (mg, g, °C) — within scope; corpus §6 demonstrates the same pattern with a coffee example. No dot product, cosine similarity, embeddings, or neural network concepts present.

previous_fail_resolved.length_bias: Previous rate 0.80 → now 0.20. Q1 correct answer shortened (16→13 words); Q2 correct answer shortened (19→14 words); Q3-D2 lengthened to 17 words; Q4-D1 and Q4-D3 each lengthened to 17 words.
previous_fail_resolved.lo3_uncovered: Q5 replaced with LO3 construction question — student reads off [160, 5, 65] from fixed slots for a cup of coffee. Previous Q5 was LO1/LO4 duplicate.

verdict: Pass — all checks clean. Positional bias 0.40 (at threshold, not over). Length bias 0.20 (down from 0.80). Distractor plausibility absurd_rate 0.00. All 5 LOs covered including LO3 (previously uncovered).
